incorrects.description: Student clicked Daily Steps table on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Check the last digit of each number in that table.

incorrects.description: Student clicked Books Read table on first attempt

steps.text: Look at the last digit of each number. Do they all end in 0 or 5?

incorrects.description: Student clicked Daily Steps table on second attempt

steps.text: Let's look at the last digits: 4,500 ends in 0, 3,200 ends in 0, 5,100 ends in 0, 2,800 ends in 0. They all end in 0 or 5, so you have multiple scale options. Which table has numbers that DON'T end in 0 or 5?

incorrects.description: Student clicked Books Read table on second attempt

steps.text: Check the last digits here: 25 ends in 5, 30 ends in 0, 35 ends in 5, 40 ends in 0. All end in 0 or 5, so multiple scales work. Which dataset has numbers that DON'T follow that pattern?

incorrects.description: Student clicked wrong table on third attempt or clicked Coins after two wrong attempts

steps.text: Let me show you how to find the answer. First, I check the Daily Steps table: 4,500, 3,200, 5,100, 2,800. All end in 0. That means multiple scales work. Next, I check Books Read: 25, 30, 35, 40. All end in 0 or 5. Multiple scales work there too. Now I check Coins Collected: 7, 12, 19, 23. None of these end in 0 or 5. When numbers don't end in 0 or 5 and are small, Scale of 2 is the only way to show them exactly. That is why Coins Collected MUST use Scale of 2.
